seoTitle: How to Convert Image URLs to Attachments in Airtable
metaDescription: Automatically turn raw image URLs into hosted Airtable attachments using an automation trigger and an Update Record action mapping.
intro: When you have a column of raw image links, Airtable's Automations can convert those URLs into actual hosted attachments automatically instead of you downloading and re-uploading each one. This guide covers setting up the trigger and the Update Record mapping that does the conversion.

steps.heading: Set up your URL and Attachment fields
steps.body: Make sure your table has a text field holding the raw image URL and a separate Attachment field to receive the converted image.

steps.heading: Open the Automations dashboard
steps.body: Navigate to the Automations tab to create a new custom trigger workflow.

steps.heading: Configure the trigger
steps.body: Set up a "When record matches conditions" trigger to detect rows containing newly entered image URLs.

steps.heading: Add the Update Record action
steps.body: Add an Update Record action step and select your active database table.

steps.heading: Map the URL into the Attachment field
steps.body: Map the dynamic image URL variable directly into the target Attachment field so Airtable downloads and hosts the image.

steps.heading: Test and activate
steps.body: Run a live test to verify the image renders correctly, then toggle the automation on for bulk conversions going forward.

faq.question: Do I need separate fields for the URL and the attachment?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a text field for the raw URL input and a separate Attachment field that the automation will populate with the hosted image.

faq.question: Can this convert a batch of existing URLs already in my table?
faq.answer: You can trigger the automation across existing records by re-triggering the match condition, effectively running bulk conversions.

faq.question: What happens if the URL is broken or invalid?
faq.answer: The conversion will likely fail for that record, so it's worth spot-checking results after running bulk conversions.

faq.question: Does the original URL field stay after conversion?
faq.answer: Yes, the automation maps the URL into the Attachment field without removing the original text field unless you delete it separately.
